ℹ️  How to Use This Guide

▾
1 · Orient
Review the flow bar above. Know which demos you're running before the customer walks in.
2 · Prep
Open required files and apps before the demo starts. Each section lists exactly what you need.
3 · Execute
Follow the steps. Click any prompt to copy it — don't retype. Paste directly into Copilot Chat.
💡 Tip: Sections are collapsed by default. Open only what you need. Click "Expand All" to open everything at once.
Part 1 — Demos
1. Copilot App ▾
1.1
Work IQ Demo
Land the value of Work IQ as the intelligence layer — Copilot knows you.
▾
🎯 Setup ▾prep notes
Open Copilot Chat (M365.com or Teams). Sign in as the demo executive. No files needed — runs entirely as a 5-prompt conversation.
📁 Files ▾
Copilot Chat only — no files required.
💬 Copy / Paste Prompts — click any prompt to copyclick to expand
PROMPT 1 For the next 5 questions, please keep answers reasonably concise. What do you know about me and my job here at Microsoft? Click to copy
PROMPT 2 What are the top 5 projects I am working on right now, and give me 1 sentence status on each Click to copy
PROMPT 3 Give me one sentence on each of the following; how I operate, spend my time, organize my work, communicate with others Click to copy
PROMPT 4 What are 5 topics that are important to me, and give a sentence on each stating my positions on them. Click to copy
PROMPT 5 What do you know about my manager and my direct reports? Give me a sentence with the latest on each. Click to copy

1.4
Agent Builder
Show how anyone can create a no-code AI agent in minutes from Copilot Chat.
▾
1.4.1 · CELA Marketing Agent — Easier
1.4.1
CELA Marketing Agent Easier
Single-URL agent that reviews marketing copy for legal compliance — quick to build, great story.
▾
🎯 Setup ▾prep notes
No pre-staging required — built entirely live from a public SharePoint URL. Just open Copilot Chat and have the prompts ready.
📁 Files ▾
Knowledge source URL (added during demo):
https://microsoft.sharepoint.com/sites/CELAWeb-Marketing/SitePages/Marketing%20Site%20Overview.aspx
▶️ Steps ▾step by step
1
In Copilot Chat, click Create agent in the right rail.
2
Enter Prompt 1 to create the legal advice agent.
3
When asked to confirm, enter Prompt 2 ("Yes").
4
Click Configure → paste the CELA Marketing SharePoint URL into the Knowledge section.
5
Click Create agent, then Go to agent and test with Prompt 3.
💬 Copy / Paste Prompts — click any prompt to copyclick to expand
PROMPT 1 — Create agent Please create a legal advice agent, to review and provide legal guidance on marketing assets. Make it clear this is just guidance, and maintain a professional tone, provide specific legal guidance, and avoid generating incorrect or misleading information. Click to copy
PROMPT 2 — Confirm Yes Click to copy
PROMPT 3 — Test agent Perform a CELA review on this value prop statement: Copilot in Microsoft 365 is the ultimate tool, guaranteed to revolutionize everyone's productivity like never before! It seamlessly integrates into your workflow, automating every mundane task and providing insights that are almost magical. With Copilot, you will always stay ahead of the curve and achieve unparalleled efficiency. Get ready to experience the future today with the best assistant ever created! Click to copy
🔗 Simulation
1.4.2 · Customer Engagement Agent — Advanced
1.4.2
Customer Engagement Agent Advanced
Multi-source agent using Teams chat + Word doc as knowledge — rich, realistic scenario.
▾
🎯 Setup ▾prep notes
Do this before the demo:
1
Create a Teams group chat with a couple of colleagues. Name it "Customer Engagement". Copy-paste the chat messages from the Zava Scenario doc into the chat so the history is there.
2
Download the Zava Logo to your machine (optional — adds polish when configuring the agent icon).
3
Have the ZavaCore Product Roadmap Word doc accessible — this will be added as a knowledge source during the demo.
📁 Files ▾
📄 Agent Builder — Zava Scenario (instructions + chat script)
Includes Teams chat transcript to pre-stage, Zava logo, and full demo script.
▶️ Steps ▾step by step
1
In Copilot Chat, click Create agent in the right rail.
2
Enter Prompt 1 below to build the Customer Engagement agent.
3
Click Configure and add three knowledge sources: ZavaCore Product Roadmap (Word doc), the Customer Engagement Teams chat, and optionally the Zava Logo.
4
Click Create agent, then Go to agent and test with Prompt 2.
💬 Copy / Paste Prompts — click any prompt to copyclick to expand
PROMPT 1 — Create agent Please create an agent to respond to travel policy questions for my organization. Please call it Travel Policy Agent Click to copy
PROMPT 2 — Test agent I need to travel internationally from Seattle to London (10 hr flight) – What is the policy for flying premium economy or business class? And what documents do I need as a US passport holder? Click to copy
Full script and additional prompts in the Zava Scenario doc ↗
🔗 Simulation
2. Core Apps — Agent Mode ▾
Agent Mode
2.2.1 · Word
Word — Copilot Agent Mode (MBR Demo)
Copilot reads your emails & chats, then rewrites your Monthly Business Review report in seconds — no copy-paste required.
▾
🎯 Setup ▾prep notes
⚠️ Allow 15 minutes for first-time setup. This demo requires pre-staged emails and chat messages in your inbox. Update all yellow-highlighted dates in the email templates if running in a month other than February 2026.
Step 1 · File Prep
1
Copy Marketing MBR.docx AND a backup copy to your personal OneDrive so you have an editable version and a clean restore copy.
2
Open Marketing MBR.docx in Word in your second Edge tab. Keep this tab ready — this is where Agent Mode runs.
3
Open M365 Copilot Chat in your first Edge tab (needed for the optional email discovery opener and V-Team priming).
Step 2 · Send Emails & Chats (see Section 4 templates)
Send all four messages to yourself before the demo. These are what Agent Mode will read to update the MBR:
Message From Purpose
Email #1 — MBR Request (optional) Your Manager Sets up the urgent request scenario. Mark as High Priority ❗
Email #2 — Messaging & Creative Colleague (Jason) Priority #2 — Behind schedule
Email #3 — MBR Priority Updates Colleague (Ze V) Launch, Digital, Customer Sell-In updates
Chat #1 — Media Update Colleague (Scott M) Media attention — on track, exceeding goals
Step 3 · Prime Copilot Memory
!
Run the V-Team Priming Prompt (Prompt 1 below) in Copilot Chat before the demo starts. This tells Copilot which names to look for when scanning emails. Without it, Agent Mode may miss updates.
📁 Files ▾
📄 Marketing MBR.docx — demo copy
📄 Marketing MBR - Backup.docx — clean restore copy
Copy both to your personal OneDrive before the demo. Use the backup to restore after each run.
▶️ Steps ▾step by step
Part 1 — Optional Opener: Email Discovery via Copilot Chat
Skip if time is limited — jump straight to Part 2 for the headline feature.
1
Go to M365 Copilot Chat (desktop or mobile). Click Start a new voice chat.
2
Say: "What is on my agenda today?" Let Copilot respond, then naturally ask: "Do I have any emails from my boss?"
3
Copilot surfaces Email #1 — the urgent MBR request. Narrate: "My manager needs this MBR updated immediately for tomorrow's leadership sync. Let me jump into Word and use Agent Mode."
Part 2 — Agent Mode in Word (Core Demo)
1
Switch to your Edge tab with Marketing MBR.docx open. Click the Copilot icon to open the Copilot pane.
2
Click Tools → select Agent Mode from the menu.
3
Paste Prompt 2 (the main Agent Mode prompt) exactly into the input field. Hit Enter.
4
Narrate while Copilot works: "Watch it scan emails, read chat messages, identify which update maps to which priority, and prepare edits — all autonomously." If Copilot pauses and asks "Should I update the document?" — respond: "Go ahead and update the document."
5
Click through the individual tracked changes — blue font highlights every edit. Show the Keep / Undo capability. When satisfied, click Keep All.
🎯 Key Talking Points
  • Agent Mode autonomously reads emails and chats — no copy-paste required
  • It understands context: knows which priorities map to which updates
  • Blue font highlights every change — review is fast and transparent
  • Status icons applied intelligently (green = on track; explained for delays)
  • Prompt to finished document: under two minutes
Demo Reset (after each run)
Option A — Version History
  1. Open Version History in Word
  2. Find the version saved before the demo
  3. Click Restore
Option B — Restore from Backup
  1. Delete Marketing MBR.docx from OneDrive
  2. Duplicate Marketing MBR - Backup.docx
  3. Rename the copy to Marketing MBR.docx
💬 Copy / Paste Prompts — click any prompt to copyclick to expand
PROMPT 1 — V-Team Priming (Copilot Chat · run BEFORE demo) Please remember that my MBR V-Team is Nirja Trivedi, Josh Addison and Callie August. Click to copy
PROMPT 2 — Agent Mode Main Prompt (paste into Word Agent Mode) This is last month's MBR. Help me update it with January data, with the latest updates from my MBR v-team (look for emails and chats) and then update the entire report, including the table, removing the December updates, and replacing with the recent ones. Only edit the last 3 columns Click to copy
PROMPT 3 — If Copilot Pauses Mid-Flow Go ahead and update the document. Click to copy
🔧 Troubleshoot ▾if things go wrong
Issue Resolution
Copilot pauses mid-flow Type "Go ahead and update the document."
Emails older than 3 weeks Resend all email and chat templates with updated dates for the current month
Copilot misses an email update Ensure the V-Team priming prompt (Prompt 1) was run and emails are in your inbox — not a subfolder
Wrong month shown in document Advance all yellow-highlighted dates in the email templates and the MBR doc template
Previous demo data appearing Delete all old messages before resetting — use the Demo Reset steps above
💡 Pro Tip: Do a full dry run before going live. This ensures email timestamps are fresh, Copilot memory is primed, and you can narrate each step confidently.
🔗 Simulation

📁 Files ▾
📊 Zava Enterprise Sales Data — SharePoint folder
Download the Excel file and copy to OneDrive before the demo.
▶️ Steps ▾step by step
1
With Agent Mode active, run Prompt 1 to filter, sort and highlight the data. Watch Copilot reason through the steps and make direct edits to the sheet. C
2
Run Prompt 2 to generate a full customer dashboard with summary metrics, category breakdown and a chart — all created autonomously in a new tab. B
💬 Copy / Paste Prompts — click any prompt to copyclick to expand
PROMPT 1 — Filter & Highlight Filter this table to only show total sales lines from Contoso, then sort all rows by Category and highlight any rows over $50K in yellow Click to copy
PROMPT 2 — Build Dashboard Create a ZavaCore launch dashboard for the Contoso account. Include total sales, order count, average order value, top category and top product. Add a sales by category bar chart. Put it in a new tab called ZavaCore Dashboard. Click to copy

3. Copilot for Teamwork ▾
3.1
Facilitator Agent in Teams
A live meeting with confederates — Copilot Facilitator tracks the agenda, assigns tasks, creates documents, and coordinates agents in real time.
▾
🎯 Setup ▾
⚠️ This demo requires 4 live confederates on a pre-scheduled Teams meeting. Allow time to brief them on the script (Section 4 of the source doc) and rehearse at least once before going live.
People Required
Name Role in Demo
NirjaMain demo lead — runs the demo, @mentions agents
JasonModerator — opens meeting, reads script, sends chat messages
JoshConfederate — speaks from script, @mentions Facilitator to add agenda item, sends chat messages
JoeConfederate — speaks from script, @mentions Facilitator to create timeline document
EmilyConfederate — speaks from script, sends chat messages
Pre-Demo Steps
1
Schedule a 60-minute Teams meeting with Jason, Josh, Joe, Emily — include the following agenda: Introductions & objectives · ZavaCore product update · ZavaCore announce dates & workback schedule · Q&A.
2
15 minutes before the demo: All 4 confederates join the meeting with cameras on and start recording. They read through the call script and follow instructions. When Nirja (demo lead) joins, everyone looks engaged at camera.
3
Update all names and dates in the script (use CTRL+H). If dates are stale, use Copilot Agent Mode to "progress all dates by X days" in the script document.
4
Ensure the Facilitator agent and EventCoordinator custom agent and the Remind 3P agent are added to the meeting before it starts.
📁 Files ▾
📄 Facilitator Demo — Resource Doc
Instruction document with the full meeting script for confederates to follow before the main demo lead joins. Remember to swap out all names (Jason, Josh, Joe, Emily) for your actual team members before sharing.
▶️ Steps ▾
Part 1 — Join & Orient the Audience
1
Before joining, show the audience the meeting agenda — point out it's a basic agenda. Narrate: "It's not a great one, but keep it in mind — it's going to matter in a moment."
2
Join the meeting. Show the attendee list — point out Facilitator is already on the call. Open the chat and widen the view.
3
Point out the agenda at the top of the meeting — Facilitator has already added it. Show the timeline bar: instructions, objectives, and time tracking per topic. Narrate: "Right now we've just wrapped up the announcement dates."
Part 2 — Agenda Item Added by Confederates
4
Scroll down in chat — show that Josh added a 15-minute agenda item for the ZavaCore press event by @mentioning Facilitator. It now appears in the agenda even though it wasn't in the original invite. Narrate this actively.
Part 3 — Document Created Live
5
Show that Joe @mentioned Facilitator to create a workback schedule document. Open it and show the audience. Narrate: "It used only the content from this meeting — not my broader work data. And it's a solid document."
Part 4 — Task Assignment
6
Use Prompt 1 or Prompt 2 (pick one based on audience) to assign a task to Jason via Facilitator. Show the task appearing in Planner with Jason assigned. Narrate: "Normally I'd have to open Planner manually — now I do it without leaving the meeting."
Part 5 — Custom Agent & 3P Agent
7
Use Prompt 3 to @mention the EventCoordinator custom agent and ask about registration numbers. This shows a custom-built agent working alongside Facilitator in the same meeting.
8
Use Prompt 4 to @mention Remind (3P agent from Microsoft Store). Set a reminder for the team. Narrate: "This is just one example from the Store — I can add more agents to extend what Teams can do."
💬 Copy / Paste Prompts — click any prompt to copyclick to expand
PROMPT 1 — Task Assignment Alt 1 (check for unassigned tasks first) @facilitator – Are there any unassigned tasks? Click to copy
PROMPT 1B — Assign task to Joe (follow-up after Alt 1) @facilitator – Assign the first task to Joe Click to copy
PROMPT 2 — Task Assignment Alt 2 (direct assign) @facilitator – Assign for Jason to share the latest draft of the ZavaCore press release Click to copy
PROMPT 3 — Custom Agent (EventCoordinator) @EventCoordinator what are the latest registration numbers for the ZavaCore press event? Click to copy
PROMPT 4 — 3P Agent (Remind) @remind remind everyone 15 minutes before our next meeting to update their weekly tracker. Click to copy
CONFEDERATE ACTION — Josh (add agenda item) @facilitator – Add a 15-minute agenda topic for discussing the ZavaCore Press Event before we get to Q&A Click to copy
CONFEDERATE ACTION — Joe (create document) @facilitator – Create a document with the timeline of activities for the next 4 weeks leading up to the press event, based on what we just discussed Click to copy
📜 Call Script ▾
💡 Share this with your confederates. Use CTRL+H to update names. Update dates using Copilot Agent Mode if needed.
Jason (Moderator): "Good morning, everyone. Welcome to our ZavaCore launch planning call. I'm Jason, Product Marketing Lead for ZavaCore. Let's start with quick introductions."
Josh: "Hi all, I'm Josh, Head of Product Development. Excited to be here as we finalize the launch details."
Joe: "Hello, Joe here, Director of Global Distribution. Looking forward to ensuring our fulfillment and retail rollout is flawless."
Emily: "Hi everyone, I'm Emily, Communications Manager. I'll be leading our press event and announcement strategy for ZavaCore."
Jason: "Thank you everyone. The objective of today's meeting is to finalize our launch plans for ZavaCore. Now that we've concluded introductions, let's talk about the Product Timing Update."
⚡ ACTION — Josh @mentions Facilitator: "@facilitator – Add a 15-minute agenda topic for discussing the ZavaCore Press Event before we get to Q&A"
Josh: "ZavaCore is on track for launch on April 17. This is our flagship smart mesh athletic apparel line, featuring graphene-infused performance knit, real-time biometric sensors, and a proprietary smartphone app. Development is complete, all SKUs have passed QA, and packaging is being finalized."
Joe: "Distribution is ready. Our facilities in St. Louis, Charlotte, Vancouver, Montreal, Bergamo, and Borås are set for regional cut, sew, test, and pack."
Emily: "On the communications side, we're preparing a major press event for launch day, plus a coordinated campaign across digital, retail, and partner channels. Let's walk through the workback schedule."
Workback Schedule (Jason leads — each person reads their milestone)
Jason: "Here's our proposed timeline with fourteen key milestones leading up to the April 17 launch. There are also two open tasks still unassigned — we'll flag those before we wrap."
Emily — Mar 10: Final QA and compliance sign-off. Every SKU, sensor, and packaging approved. All certifications confirmed.
Josh — Mar 13: Bulk production and regional fulfillment begins. Woodinville HQ ships to US, Canada, and European facilities.
Joe — Mar 15: First internal field test with wearable mesh and sensor suite. Live data capture and app sync validation.
Emily — Mar 17: Finalize retail packaging design and submit for print.
Jason — Mar 19: Allocate inventory for retail and online. Micro retail stores get initial demo inventory.
Josh — Mar 21: Virtual onboarding session for regional fulfillment leads.
Joe — Mar 23: All marketing assets finalized. Website and e-commerce pages updated.
Emily — Mar 25: Full-stack integration test across ZavaCore app, cloud sync, and analytics dashboard.
Jason — Mar 28: Press event invitations sent. Social media teasers begin rolling out.
Josh — Mar 30: Final legal and compliance checkpoint.
Joe — Apr 1: Retail staff training and demo station setup.
Emily — Apr 6: Partner and athlete seeding. B2B partners briefed.
Jason — Apr 17: Launch day! Global press event, live demos, online and retail sales go live.
Jason: "Before we move to the final topic, there are two open tasks: First, finalize the athlete list for April 6 seeding. Second, confirm demo station setup requirements for the press event and micro retail stores by April 1. Both still unassigned."
Jason: "Alright, let's transition to our final topic — the press event…"
⚡ (NO MORE AUDIO — everyone mute)
⚡ ACTION — Joe @mentions Facilitator in chat: "@facilitator – Create a document with the timeline of activities for the next 4 weeks leading up to the press event, based on what we just discussed"
Chat Messages (paste in chat while muted):
Joe: Just got confirmation from Bergamo — regional teams are ready for the cut, sew, and pack ops. Excited to see the first demo units!
Emily: I'll share the draft press release by end of day. If anyone has key athlete testimonials to include, DM me!
Josh: QA passed on all SKUs! 🎉 Thanks to everyone who helped with the sensor integration. Packaging update coming soon.
Jason: Love the idea of athlete seeding — maybe we can get some early social posts from the teams once they receive their kits?
Josh: Can we get a quick status check on the demo station setups for San Francisco and Dallas? Want to make sure we're on track for April 1.
🔗 Simulation

6. Biz Apps ▾
6.1
Sales Agent in Microsoft 365
Show how Copilot surfaces rich sales context and accelerates seller productivity — all within M365.
▾
🎯 Setup ▾
1
Reboot your PC in the morning before the session.
2
Set display to 16:9 aspect ratio, 1080×1920 at 125% scale. You may need to reset this when connecting to production equipment.
3
Ensure ZoomIt is running and you have practiced using it.
4
Open the simulation link below. Load it in Tab 5 before the session.
📁 Files ▾
No files required — simulation runs standalone.
▶️ Steps ▾
1
Press R (or hit reload) to reset to the start of the demo at any time. Press F11 to go full screen.
2
Click Sales on the left rail.
3
Scroll down to show all the rich detail in the sales view.
4
Click "Accelerate Sales with Copilot: WGB Sync".
5
Click "Ask Sales" on the left side to add a prompt. End demo here — transition back to speaker.

Add copy/paste prompts here.

Part 1 — Seller View
1
Hit reload to reset the demo. Click on "Your Inquiry" text box to populate the question, then hit Submit.
2
Click (or press next) to jump to D365 Sales Hub for Sales Leads. Highlight that this is a hot lead.
3
Scroll down to show detail. Click "How was this generated".
Part 2 — Admin / Agent Config View
4
Click Sales (bottom left), then Create & manage agents, then Sales Qualification Agent.
5
Click Handoff Criteria and scroll down to show detail.
6
Click Research, then Manage to show agent knowledge settings.

6.4
D365 Service Agent
For service agents — Copilot surfaces full case context, sentiment tracking, and AI-suggested resolutions in real time.
▾
🎯 Setup ▾
Load the simulation link below in Tab 8 before the session. Animations in this demo are slow — have your talk track ready to fill the time.
📁 Files ▾
📄 Demo 3.3 — D365 Service Agent Script (SharePoint)
▶️ Steps ▾
1
Hit reload to reset the demo. Click anywhere on the screen to begin.
2
Describe the environment. Show the notification in the top right and click Accept. Zoom in.
3
Walk the audience through both sides: Left (communication card, sentiment, chat history, summary) and Right (Copilot assistance panel).
4
Click the bottom left chat box to start the chat. Note: animations are slow — maintain your talk track as the screen animates.
5
Click "Delivery frequency" (top right), then hit Send (bottom left). Point out the updated sentiment as the conversation animates.
6
Let the animation complete until the customer has responded "Yes".
7
Click "Request solution" on the right side, zoom in, click Edit, and click Send.
8
Click Resolve Case to close the demo.
💬 Promptsclick to expand
Add copy/paste prompts here.
🔗 Simulation
Part 2 — Demo Tools
Demo Tools ▾
7.1
ZoomIt
Screen zoom and annotation tool for live demos.
▾
ZoomIt is a free Microsoft Sysinternals tool that lets you zoom in on any part of the screen while demoing — without switching away from your application. It runs silently in the system tray and activates instantly with a hotkey. Practice it extensively before using it with a customer so it feels natural. 📥 Download from Microsoft Sysinternals ↗
📸 Visual Reference
Copilot Chat interface — shows the left nav areas (New chat, Search, Library, Create, Frontier) and pinned Agents panel. Click to enlarge.
One-Time Setup
1
Download and launch ZoomIt. Run the ZoomIt application (not ZoomIt64 or ZoomIt64a).
2
Open the Options menu — accessible from the system taskbar tray icon (click the caret to find it).
3
On the Zoom tab, configure:
A. Set Zoom Toggle to Ctrl + 2
B. Set zoom magnification level to 1.5 — optimal for Copilot demos
C. Check "Run ZoomIt when Windows starts"
4
On the LiveZoom tab, configure:
A. Set LiveZoom Toggle to Ctrl + 1 — easy to trigger with your left hand while demoing
B. Click OK
7
To exit ZoomIt, close it from the system tray — right-click the icon and select Exit.
Shortcut Reference
ShortcutFeatureTip
Ctrl + 1 Live Zoom Get familiar with how cursor movement affects screen focus during live zoom. Use Ctrl+Up / Ctrl+Down to adjust zoom level while active.
Ctrl + 2
(then click)		 Snapshot Zoom After taking the snapshot — hold Ctrl to draw boxes, hold Ctrl + Shift to draw arrows. Press Esc to exit.
⚠️ Practice first. Use ZoomIt extensively before going live with a customer — the goal is for zoom and annotation to feel completely natural and uninterrupted during the demo flow.
7.2
Auto Hotkey
Custom keyboard shortcuts for writing prompts.
▾
Auto Hotkey lets you use F1, F2, F3 etc. to instantly type long prompts during live demos — no typos, no delays. Download the ABS pre-built hotkey file and you're ready in minutes.
📥 Download Auto Hotkey Installer 📄 Download ABS AHK File
One-Time Setup
1
Download and run the Auto Hotkey installation file.
2
Create an Auto Hotkey folder somewhere on your OneDrive.
3
Right-click the folder → "Pin to Quick Access" so it shows in your Favorites sidebar. B
4
Copy the ABS AHK file into that folder.
5
Double-click the AHK file to run it. Make sure your FN key is lit up.
6
Go to any prompt box or text editor → press F1. It should type out the first hotkey prompt. ✅
Customising & Managing
TaskHow
Edit promptsRight-click the AHK file → "Edit in Notepad". Edit the prompt text only — do not edit the F1::Send, part. Save, then double-click to re-run. C
Reload after editingRight-click the Auto Hotkey icon in the system tray → "Reload this script" D
Disable shortcutsRight-click the Auto Hotkey icon in the system tray → Exit
Multiple AHK filesIf using multiple hotkey files (e.g. different functions), you must Exit the others from the taskbar first — they will compete with each other.
Test before every demoPress F# keys to verify they work. Remember the F# numbers so you don't trigger the wrong script.
💡 Pro Tip: Use physical tape labels on your F-keys to remember which shortcut triggers which demo prompt. A
Your Hotkey Mapping
F-KeyDemo / PromptNotes
F1Add prompt / demo nameAdd note
F2Add prompt / demo nameAdd note
F3Add prompt / demo nameAdd note
F4Add prompt / demo nameAdd note
Add more rows as needed — fill in during Edit mode
📸 Visual References (A–D)
Image A — F-key tape labels — label your keyboard for quick reference
Image B — "Pin to Quick Access" — step 3 of setup
Image C — AHK file in Notepad — F-key to prompt mapping
Image D — System tray menu — Reload or Exit the script
7.3
Windows Clipboard History
Pre-load longer prompts into clipboard history for instant paste.
▾
Windows Clipboard History lets you copy multiple strings at once and paste any of them on demand with Win+V. It's especially useful for long prompts that are too slow to type with Auto Hotkeys — pre-load them before your demo and paste instantly.
One-Time Setup — Enable Clipboard History
⚡ Quickest way: Press Win + V — if not enabled, Windows will show a prompt with a "Turn On" button. Click it and you're done. A
Alternative (via Settings): Settings → System → Clipboard → toggle Clipboard History on.
How to Use
1
Copy each prompt you need using Ctrl+C — copy them all before the demo starts.
2
Press Win+V in any text field to open the clipboard panel and see all copied items. B
3
Click any item to paste it, use the pin icon to keep favourites across restarts, or the … menu to delete individual items.
4
Clear all (button in top-right of the panel) before each demo to start with a clean history.
💡 Pro Tip: Use this for long prompts that are too slow for Auto Hotkeys — pin them in clipboard history so they survive restarts and are always ready.
📸 Visual References (A–B)
Image A — Win+V "Turn On" prompt — one-click activation
Image B — Clipboard history panel — select, pin or delete copied strings
